summary: A small rectangular bunker-style structure with a sunken foundation surrounded by a white concrete border. The main body features brick walls with a dark wood plank roof that slopes inward. Multiple small rectangular windows are visible on the front facade. The building sits in an excavated pit with dirt walls and is accessed from ground level.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with inset roof and sunken foundation

rebuild_hints: Excavate a rectangular pit approximately 12×8 blocks, 3-4 blocks deep
Build concrete/white block border around the perimeter at ground level
Construct brick walls inside the pit, approximately 3 blocks high
Add small rectangular windows (approximately 2×2 blocks) on front facade, spaced evenly
Install dark wood plank roof that slopes inward toward center
Roof should overhang slightly beyond walls on all sides
Add stone brick or concrete trim between wall and roof line
Ensure symmetrical window placement along front face
uncertainties: Exact interior dimensions and layout not visible
Precise window count and spacing uncertain from angles shown
Roof slope angle and exact construction method unclear
Whether structure has interior rooms or is single chamber
Specific concrete block type used for border
